industry: D2C + retail sports and casual footwear
product_description: Indian sports and casual footwear brand selling sneakers, running shoes, walking shoes, sports shoes, and casual shoes for men, women, and kids. Price range ₹499–₹3,499. Sold via D2C website, Amazon, Flipkart, Myntra, and 20,000+ multi-brand retail outlets across India. Manufactured in India (Ganaur, Haryana facility). Publicly listed on NSE/BSE since 2022.
target_audience: Mass-market and aspirational-mid-market Indians aged 16-35 in tier-2 and tier-3 cities, plus tier-1 value seekers. Students, young professionals, gym-goers, and daily-wear buyers who want sporty, trend-forward footwear at ₹800-₹2,000 sweet spot. They compare on price, style, and brand trust — not technical running specs.
unique_selling_points: Sharp price-to-style ratio — Puma-like looks at half the price
Made-in-India manufacturing at scale (own factory, not outsourced)
Deepest distribution in tier-2/3 — available where Nike and Adidas aren't
Kids and women's ranges beyond the typical men-only sports SKUs
focus_product: The Campus sneaker that launched in MARCH 2026 (very recent — identify the exact SKU name from live news/PR/social by searching 'Campus Shoes March 2026 launch', 'Campus Activewear new sneaker March 2026', campusshoes.com new-arrivals, and press releases from March 2026. Use web_search with the explicit year 2026 since older results are NOT relevant. The campaign will be built around THIS specific SKU. If no March 2026 launch is verifiable from live data, state 'evidence not found for March 2026 launch' explicitly in why_it_matters and fall back to the newest identifiable SKU.
